candidates.why: Appears as a component beneath the operating-expense D&A summary row, located in the operating expense section after gross profit rather than within or alongside the selected COGS bucket 'Cost of Revenues - Total'. No explicit COGS-specific D&A row is shown.
Also appears as a component beneath the operating-expense D&A summary row and not as a COGS-specific line. It looks like operating overhead D&A rather than a separate cost-of-revenues add-on.
Listed under the operating-expense D&A grouping, with no indication it is tied specifically to COGS. It does not appear as a separate COGS-level line to add to the selected COGS bucket.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortization row is identified as belonging to cost of revenues. The visible D&A rows sit in the operating expense section below gross profit, so conservatively treat COGS D&A as already embedded in 'Cost of Revenues - Total' or otherwise not separately addable from this excerpt.

candidates.why: Pure D&A component shown as a separate operating-expense line outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Other external costs', 'Marketing expenses', 'Employee Costs'). Since the visible summary row includes impairments, this pure component is safer to add separately than the mixed summary.
Pure D&A component shown separately in the operating section and not part of the selected XSGA component rows. It should be added separately if constructing SG&A plus D&A.
This appears to be a D&A component under the same operating-expense grouping, but the preview shows no values and it is less clear whether it contributes in the relevant periods.

Separate same-level operating-expense line outside the selected XSGA component rows, but it mixes D&A with impairment amounts, so using it would risk violating the instruction to ignore impairments and may overstate XSGA_DA.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists only of 'Other external costs', 'Marketing expenses', and 'Employee Costs'. The statement shows additional operating-expense D&A separately below those rows, so D&A is not already embedded in the selected XSGA rows. The mixed summary row 'Depreciation, Amortization and Impairments' is avoided because it includes impairment. Therefore use the pure D&A component rows only. 'Amortization of Brands' is not selected because the excerpt shows no visible amounts.
